author: @Superbassmonk
text: Hello, I know this is from years ago but I am a Mech Engieenering student and my final year project is to manufacture a stretchable conductor. I am wanting to create a semi solid ink/paste using graphite which will act as the filler to a elastomer tube and in searching for solutions I came across this video. I didnt catch what you added to the powder to make it into the ink around 1:13 and I would like to try and recreate what you have done here; would you be able to let me know what it is you used? My email is tfinlay756@yahoo.co.uk, I would greatly appreciate any opinions you may have on this subject and would happily credit yourself in my final report

author: @mayad8509
text: pyrolytic

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: maya d no mate - it's mined vein graphite

replies.author: @mayad8509
replies.text: is that same as pyrolytic graphite, why isnt everybody living off grid bynowthis cracy

author: @74Persia
text: Hello Robert thank you for your great videos .I guess that the conductivity strongly depends on compression and smoothness  of the layer  with minimal layer interconnection (like of graphene) so it can be used only on single crystal substrate .copper and silver inks can handle layer imperfection by conducting in 3d . please let us know  about your farther experiments on this .I havent noticed any diamagnetic behavior how thick it should be ? is this inkjet able with etyle cellulose?  thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I doubt you could get it to inkjet as you would have to break the structure down to the nanometer level and the diamagnetism is bound to be influenced by sheet size - smoothness won't have an effect but defects will - so being conformal is the issue along with minimum interconnects

replies.author: @74Persia
replies.text: do you think inkjet with heat press afterward will work? I have a suggestion you may also sell your experiment in pdf downloadable format  of related categories .or some other ways to make your work more sustainable one last question I need to minimize friction between a magnet and a surface . tried this graphite layer to repel the magnet but it didnt help to lower the current needed to move the magnet. is this dimagnetic at room temperature? thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
74Persia
yes it is diamagnetic at room temp and i don't think heat pressing will work unless you get it to a stupidly high temp and a huge pressure - but it is probably worth an experiment - so give it a go and see - The amount of work involved in putting our PDFs is huge and I am unlikely to do it as the cost benefit ratio is just too small

author: @eveberry1798
text: BTW have you tried putting this in an inkjet printer and seeing if you can actually print with it? Might need some solvent to help it along.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I haven't - inkjet printing is not something i spend a lot of time thinking about. Most bulk printing is flexographic or screen and i spend quite a lot of time on that - but inkjet is too niche an application at the moment

author: @eveberry1798
text: Have you seen this: http://phys.org/news/2011-11-graphene-ink-ink-jet-electronic-components.html

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have thanks - but thank you for thinking of me

author: @mikehebblethwaite3693
text: Is that painted on the conductive side of the glass or the none conductive side?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: dude - it's painted on glass - there is no 'conductive side'

replies.author: @mikehebblethwaite3693
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Put your multi meter on ohms and press 1 cm apart on one side of the glass then flip it and try the other side. I make solar panels and one side is conductive. Hope that helps

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
Mike Hebblethwaite
mate - i don't know what to say - what you are saying is true if you buy ITO glass - if you buy glass in the middle of Sri Lanka meant for windows - it is not ITO coated and NOT conductive - hope that helps

author: @Wrongrealities
text: Do you think you can make a battery or solar panel using 3 different metals painted over each other?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's basically what a solar cell is - so yes if it was material instead of metal

i'm a bit worried about this.  To be so malliable and conductive i'd assume this ore contains a lot of *lead*.
But i'm glad you know better and you know what you're doing.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Hiya mate, well the basics as you know are pretty simple and an organic base can be plated if first covered with a conductive layer. I have done this with a whole range of things and a couple of methods including platinum seeding for electroless deposition, which i know you are not even going to consider lol. But i have used conductive inks and I will try and get round to doing a video on it for you.

author: @picnet
text: Curiously the pg + parafin + gum arabic mix exhibited lower resistance than just the pg + gum arabic alone.

author: @picnet
text: Parafin + blender reduced pg sieved though stainless mesh rubbed onto paper has poor resistance compared to same mix in gum arabic (flat side) which is < 70 ohms/sq cm. Compressing and rubbing the compound in graphite dust (as picked up in the table experiment above?) reduces this resistance to < 46 ohms/sq cm which is still far worse than the sub ohm levels demonstrated here. Paper in the parafin+pg dust with a flat shiny surface is around 80 ohms. Flatland plays a big role here.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: like I say I am unlikely to get to applications with this stuff for a while - I am hoping others will pick it up and run with it. My mind is on homopolar motors from this stuff - mostly because the guys who own the company are engineers and that is what they are interested in. I go home in a couple of weeks and I should be able to do some free time stuff but it is likely to be on AZO coating glass. I try to do the basics as tools for others who want to experiment but don't know how to proceed

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: there is a link to the site in the description - if not this vid then certainly in the earlier ink or diamagnetic vids

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: It is very cool stuff - I am taking a break from general research while I work on this stuff for a bit but I would think AZO coating glass would be more of a way forward on this and I will return to that later - I have done some prep work on this which is why the zinc acetate vid is up but work called me away. Anyway this stuff is still early days and we are looking at properties only not applications as yet. It'll be a while before I get into applications - I was hoping others would run with it

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I know - we are very excited by it as you can imagine - I feel a bit like an explorer lol

Exciting work.
